page: Amazon Web Services i3.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 2 threads per core operating at 2.3 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 45 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 122.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, fall into the bottom 10% category. However, multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory read bandwidth is average for both small and large blocks, though large-block write bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also show weak to poor performance, while multi-threaded decompression is weak.

In terms of workload execution, the instance achieves average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Large language model inference speeds are average for small models but drop to weak for prompt processing on medium models. Given the high local NVMe SSD storage density relative to the compute profile, this instance is structured for storage-heavy workloads, databases, and caching layers that require high local capacity, though it presents a tradeoff with lower single-core processing efficiency.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 122.0 GB of RAM. It includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks are poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU metrics, memory read bandwidth, and database operations perform at an average level within the middle 50%. The instance delivers average throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving. This hardware profile, characterized by high local storage capacity relative to compute resources, is designed for storage-intensive workloads, databases, and caching applications that benefit from direct NVMe access rather than raw single-threaded CPU speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 122 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 122 GB RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining 3.8 TB of local NVMe SSD capacity with balanced multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 16 vCPUs and 122.0 GB of system memory
Includes 3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps
Exhibits lower single-core and single-threaded compression efficiency
Suited for storage-intensive databases and caching layers
